title: Unraveling the sensory metabolome of blueberries: An integrated metabolomics and machine learning approach across cultivars and geographical origins.
author_string: Liu, Di, Liu, Xinjuan, Hu, Qian, Xing, Ranran
abstract: Consumer-driven blueberry quality improvement requires a deeper understanding of how metabolic composition influences sensory perception. This study integrates untargeted metabolomics and machine learning to identify biomarker metabolites shaping sensory attributes across blueberry cultivars and geographical origins. Metabolite profiling of four cultivars from four areas revealed significant cultivar- and geographical-dependent differences in metabolic patterns of volatile and non-volatile compounds. Through a combination of correlation analysis and random forest modeling, 27 volatiles and 57 non-volatiles were identified as biomarkers for color, mouthfeel, and flavor attributes, and their predictive validity was further evaluated using support vector regression. These biomarkers explained up to 86 % of the variance in specific sensory attributes and 66 % of overall liking, demonstrating both explanatory strength and predictive robustness. This study provides a data-driven strategy for optimizing blueberry quality through targeted biomarker-based approaches. The proposed metabolomics and machine learning framework establishes a scalable model for improving metabolite quality traits in plant-based foods, aligning with evolving consumer preferences.
